evals.prompt: cria um PDF explicando Árvore Binária de Busca com simulado, quero estudar pra prova
evals.expected_output: PDF file with cover, BST explanation, C99 code with struct No, ASCII tree diagram, complexity table, and simulado with 5+ questions and commented gabarito

assertions.description: PDF file was generated and has more than 1 page
PDF text contains C99 struct definition (struct No or typedef struct)
PDF contains an ASCII tree diagram showing nodes with / \ characters or [value] boxes
Simulado section has 5 or more questions
PDF contains a GABARITO section with commented answers
PDF covers inserir, buscar, and remover operations (or equivalent Portuguese/English terms)

evals.prompt: me faz um material de estudo sobre Deadlocks pra SO — as 4 condições de Coffman, estratégias de tratamento, tudo isso. Quero com simulado no final
evals.expected_output: PDF with cover, Coffman's 4 conditions, detection/prevention/avoidance strategies, Banker's Algorithm mention, simulado with dissertative and multiple-choice questions, and gabarito

assertions.description: PDF file was generated and has more than 1 page
PDF mentions all 4 Coffman conditions: mutual exclusion, hold-and-wait, no preemption, circular wait
PDF mentions Banker's Algorithm (Algoritmo do Banqueiro or Banker)
PDF covers at least 2 of: detection, prevention, avoidance (detecção, prevenção, esquiva)
Simulado section has 4 or more questions

evals.prompt: gera um PDF de pilha (LIFO) — implementação em C, push/pop/peek, com questões de código no simulado tipo completar a função
evals.expected_output: PDF with cover, LIFO concept, C99 Stack implementation (push/pop/peek/isEmpty), ASCII stack diagram, simulado with code-completion questions and gabarito

assertions.description: PDF file was generated and has more than 1 page
PDF contains push, pop, and peek functions in C code
PDF contains an ASCII stack diagram (TOP arrow or vertical stack representation)
Simulado contains at least one code-completion question (tipo: codigo)
PDF contains a GABARITO section with commented answers
PDF explicitly explains LIFO principle (Last In, First Out)
